photo_read: May 12 front-support photos and 21:29-21:32 measurement photos show the radiator plane, nearby fan/pulley clearance, vertical upright/top tab, lower/front support opening, and removed-radiator side/lower brackets. Previous temporary wire/loop support path is still part of the read; exact hole diameters, hole-centre map, metal thickness, and isolator contact still need template confirmation.
decision: Fabricate or repair a serviceable two-side radiator retention set using the visible upright/lower support/radiator flange as the first design datum where the metal is sound.
next_action: Create labelled template/sketch from the new measurements: radiator side/lower tabs, upright/top tab, base bolts, hole diameters, hole centres, edge distances, lower support contact, isolator/rubber contact area, and fan clearance.
notes: Keep radiator plane unless dry-fit proves movement required; do not hard-clamp radiator tank or core.

photo_read: The May 12 front-support views plus removed-radiator measurement set show both radiator-side bracket layouts and the in-vehicle front-support/upright envelope, but they still do not prove a released symmetrical two-side retention bracket without a labelled dry-fit.
decision: Verify side-to-side retention before releasing the radiator bracket design.
next_action: Use the removed-radiator measurement photos to mark both side bracket pickup options, then photograph the opposite side with the radiator dry-fitted and a labelled hole-centre/offset sketch.
notes: Do not assume the existing one visible side represents the complete mounting set.

component_or_function: Lower radiator support or lower pickup holes
photo_read: May 12 images show a front/lower support hole field and the new measurement photos add lower radiator tab and front-opening context; isolator style, hole condition, metal thickness, and lower load path are still not fully measured.
decision: Use or repair lower pickups only after thickness, hole shape, and rubber/isolator fit are checked.
next_action: Clean the lower support area, inspect for oval holes or thin metal, then record hole diameter, pitch, edge distance, metal thickness, rubber contact area, and lower bracket template fit.
notes: Lower support work must not trap rust or block drain/service access.

component_or_function: Radiator, fan, belt, hose, and bonnet clearance envelope
photo_read: Fan, belt, hose, radiator, alternator, steering, and exhaust-side packaging are visible enough to set design constraints; the 21:31-21:32 measurement photos add upright/opening/fan-envelope readings, but final dry-fit must confirm no fan, hose, bonnet, steering, or service-clearance conflict.
decision: Treat current radiator plane and fan clearance as a release constraint for any bracket repair; keep the battery stand/carrier clear of steering, alternator, exhaust, and hose movement.
next_action: Transcribe the closest fan-to-radiator and support-opening measurements into the template sheet, then re-check after any bracket dry-fit with the radiator and isolators installed.
notes: Any bracket that pulls the radiator toward the fan, stresses the upper hose, or crowds steering/exhaust/service access fails release.

photo_read: Battery location/top restraint is known from earlier overview photos; May 12 battery-side context helps show surrounding structure and clearance, but the installed battery, top clamp path, and bonnet clearance still need a ruler check.
decision: Inspect and upgrade the top restraint only after the tray base support is proven strong enough.
next_action: Measure battery length, width, height, terminal orientation, top clamp path, bonnet clearance, and any existing clamp holes.
notes: Clamp must not touch terminals or rely on a weak tray edge.

component_or_function: Battery tray base, tray feet, and support legs
photo_read: May 12 photo shows battery-side rail/plate context and nearby clearance constraints, so a stand/tray support concept can start; tray underside, feet, cracks, welds, and exact lower pickup points are still not proven.
decision: Build or reinforce a battery stand tied into real structure rather than flat unsupported sheet; candidate load path is tray perimeter plus lower/inner support pickups, separated from the radiator bracket load path.
next_action: Capture top, side, front, rear, and underside tray photos with ruler; include existing legs, cracks, corrosion, drain path, possible lower brace route, and mark candidate lower landing points.
notes: This remains the main battery-holder release gap; the new image improves location and clearance read but does not release dimensions.

component_or_function: Battery-side MIDI fuse plate and master cutoff carrier pickups
photo_read: Battery location and MIDI/cutoff hardware are documented; May 12 context shows nearby structure/clearance, but no vehicle-side carrier pickup has been measured or released.
decision: Design a removable carrier that uses tray support or adjacent structure, not the battery tray skin alone.
next_action: Mock up a 190 x 150 mm MIDI plate plus cutoff body near the installed battery; photograph cable lug sweep, cable exits, service access, bonnet clearance, and proposed tray-stand pickup points.
notes: Keep this vehicle-side carrier separate from the existing MIDI Rev C plate package.

component_or_function: Front support auxiliary holes, tabs, and clip points
photo_read: Front support and crossmember holes/tabs are visible as context, including the May 12 hole fields and the new upright/opening measurement photos, but each point is not yet assigned to a proven function.
decision: Do not weld generic auxiliary tabs; assign each hole or tab to radiator, line, harness, earth, exhaust, cable support, battery stand, or no-refit before work.
next_action: Photograph each front support hole/tab with a label and assign keep, repair, fabricate, remove non-baseline, or unknown; attach hole-centre and edge-distance measurements where the radiator bracket will land.
notes: This row captures the front-support part of Akbar's estimated auxiliary bracket additions.

component_or_function: Added structure layout for radiator retention and battery stand
photo_read: The May 12 images now show enough to start the structure concept with measurements: radiator retention can reference the removed radiator side/lower brackets plus visible upright/front-lower support and fan/opening envelope; the battery stand remains a separate tray/stand load path tied into lower/inner support metal, not thin sheet or the radiator bracket alone.
decision: Create two template cards: one radiator upright/side strap using isolated radiator contact, and one battery tray stand with lower/inner pickups and gussets. Shared nearby pickup zones are acceptable only if the radiator and battery loads remain separated.
next_action: Create two template cards: one radiator upright/side strap using isolated radiator contact and the new measurements, and one battery tray stand with lower/inner pickups and gussets. Photograph both in place with ruler before cutting steel.
notes: Do not combine radiator support and battery mass into one flexible bracket; common structure is allowed only with separated load paths and no fan/steering/exhaust interference.
